title: Plugins remove (uninstall) + final cross-panel polish / honesty pass

tests.summary: 4 passed — drop manifest entry + remove cache dir UNDER plugins/ (preserving version+siblings); PATH GUARD never removes an installPath outside <home>/plugins/; not-found (direct + dispatch); dispatch round-trip returns plugin:<key>

suite.summary: 423 passed, 2 skipped, 0 failed — whole suite minus the env-dependent live-gateway test (the CRUD wave added 30 specs across mcp-writer/quarters-crud/primitive-files/hooks-crud/plugin-writer)

e2e.summary: 9 passed, 1 skipped (the pre-existing unconditional armory-install.spec skip). The 5 quarters-crud specs (mcp/skills/scripts/hooks/plugins) + the prior settings/memory/quarters/quarters-logs specs all green — no regression from the sandbox-seed/blurb/panel changes

by: vision screenshots c5-plugins-list.png + c5-plugins-confirm.png vs the shell
designAudit.notes: Plugins row: plugin id + version, LOOSE badge, Remove (alarm-red), no Edit/Promote (correct — remove-only). The uninstall confirm clearly states it edits Claude Code's installed_plugins.json + cache and that a running Claude Code may need a restart; reinstall via /plugin. Matches the shell; no emoji

video.note: Covered by the VERIFIED consolidated 'Manage everything from Quarters' walkthrough (11 beats, all vision-verified, run not flagged). This slice's own re-runnable committed e2e is the per-slice correctness gate.

blockers: Plugin INSTALL (from a marketplace) is NOT built — it needs marketplace resolution + a git clone and stays with Claude Code's own /plugin (gated on SP6). Only uninstall (remove) ships this slice.

Plugin remove is a cooperative-ownership edit of installed_plugins.json (the documented source of truth — no separate enabled-plugins list, no marketplace auto-reinstall, verified by inspecting the real ~/.claude/plugins structure). Same caveat as the settings.json surface: a RUNNING Claude Code may rewrite the manifest on exit, so the confirm dialog warns to restart. Not a silent risk.
notes: The advisor's pre-build safety check resolved IN FAVOUR of building: installed_plugins.json is the source of truth (no enabledPlugins in settings.json; known_marketplaces.json only lists availability), so a manifest-entry removal is safe + reversible (reinstall re-clones). plugin-writer.ts removes the entry (preserving version+siblings) and path-guards the cache-dir cleanup to within <home>/plugins/. The plugins surface is remove-only (Editor optional in the registry; creatable false). Honesty pass: blurbs/labels for mcps/hooks/plugins updated to state the new CRUD capabilities.
